ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for longan flaky pastry; 'pastry food product' (FOODON_03315091) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pastries and cakes) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for longan-filled Chinese flaky pastry.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for longan flaky pastry; category is not clinically coded in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One longan cake from the box of 10 weighs approximately 45 g and constitutes a single serving as a snack or dessert accompaniment.

nutrient_source.title: Cookies, butter, commercially prepared (USDA SR Legacy 18158) adapted for Chinese flaky pastry with longan filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Din Tai Fung longan flaky pastry. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for butter shortcrust pastry (USDA FDC 172775, 'Cookies, butter, commercially prepared') with adjustments for a longan fruit filling (approximately 15% of total weight). Longan fruit contributes approximately 60 kcal/100g, 0.8g protein, 0.1g fat, 15.1g carbohydrate per USDA FDC 167762. The blended estimate reflects a pastry shell (~85%) and longan filling (~15%) composition. Energy cross-checked against USDA FDC 172775 (457 kcal/100g for butter pastry) and adjusted downward to reflect the lower-energy longan filling component.

gi_source.title: Shortbread / butter biscuit GI analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Din Tai Fung longan flaky pastry. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the closest analogous food: shortbread/butter biscuit (GI ~55, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables). The longan filling is a low-GI fruit component (longan GI ~50) and does not materially alter the overall GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (55 × 24.75g) / 100 ≈ 13.6 g, where available carbohydrate per 45g serving = 55.0 × 0.45 = 24.75g.

hypertension.guidance: Each 45 g serving provides approximately 81 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to occasional consumption and monitor cumulative sodium from other dietary sources throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 55 and approximately 24.8 g available carbohydrate per 45 g serving (GL ≈ 13.6), individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence, pair it with a low-GI meal, and monitor postprandial blood glucose response carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and trans fat content from butter and shortening
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 45 g serving contributes approximately 4.9 g saturated fat and 0.09 g trans fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional servings and prioritise unsaturated fat sources in the overall diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Din Tai Fung Longan Cakes (桂圓酥, Guì Yuán Sū) are a signature gift-box pastry from the internationally acclaimed Taiwanese restaurant chain Din Tai Fung. Each box contains 10 individually crafted flaky shortcrust cakes filled with wood-smoked Tainan longan. The longan fruit is gently smoked over wood to concentrate its natural floral sweetness before being encased in a light, buttery, multi-layered pastry shell. The result is a refined balance of delicate sweetness, subtle smokiness, and a crisp yet melt-in-the-mouth texture. Nutritionally, each cake (approximately 45 g) provides around 189 kcal, 9.2 g fat (of which 4.9 g is saturated), 24.8 g carbohydrate, and 9.9 g sugar. The low glycemic index (estimated ~55) and moderate glycemic load (~13.6 per serving) mean that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this product only occasionally and monitor blood glucose. The saturated fat and trans fat content from butter and shortening warrants caution for those managing cardiovascular disease. Sodium per serving is approximately 81 mg, making it a moderate concern for hypertension when consumed alongside other sodium-containing foods. The product is sold as a premium souvenir or gift item at Din Tai Fung outlets in Singapore, including Marina Bay Link Mall, and is priced at SGD 44.80 per box of 10. It is not suitable for vegans, those with gluten intolerance, or those avoiding dairy or egg allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for Din Tai Fung longan flaky pastry in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy butter shortcrust pastry data (FDC 172775) blended with USDA longan fruit data (FDC 167762) in an approximate 85:15 shell-to-filling ratio. GI is analogously estimated from shortbread/butter biscuit data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy FDC 172775 (butter cookies/shortcrust pastry) and FDC 167762 (longan fruit, raw) in an 85:15 weight ratio. No exact Din Tai Fung product nutritional panel was available from an approved source at time of processing. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this estimation. 3. GI is an analogous estimate based on shortbread/butter biscuit (GI ~55) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement for this product exists. This GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; Din Tai Fung Singapore outlets are not certified halal as of the knowledge cutoff. 5. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Snacks' as this is a finished pastry confection sold as a gift/snack item, not a staple grain product. 6. Serving size of 45 g is estimated as total box weight divided by 10 pieces; actual individual piece weight may vary slightly. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy analogous source and should be treated as approximate.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' to accurately reflect the `glycemic_index` of 55, resolving the Q4 failure from the previous iteration.
Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `llm_training.natural_language_description` to reflect the corrected GI category.
Reclassified food_group from 'Grains and staples' to 'Snacks' to accurately reflect the product category.
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 and Q1 for Chinese cuisine served in Singapore.
Estimated nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy FDC 172775 and FDC 167762 with documented blending methodology.
Set GI to 55 (low) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database shortbread/butter biscuit data; documented estimation basis.
Set vitamin_d_mcg to -1 and documented in notes as not reported in source.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency.
Ensured all three health context conditions present with specific clinical guidance.
Confirmed no invented ontology codes; all codes are real FoodOn and FoodEx2 entries used as closest_parent with gap notes.
